2603.24470.title: Counting Without Numbers \& Finding Without Words
2603.24470.paper_summary: Every year, 10 million pets enter shelters, separated from their families. Despite desperate searches by both guardians and lost animals, 70% never reunite, not because matches do not exist, but because current systems look only at appearance, while animals recognize each other through sound. We ask, why does computer vision treat vocalizing species as silent visual objects? Drawing on five decades of cognitive science showing that animals perceive quantity approximately and communicate identity acoustically, we present the first multimodal reunification system integrating visual and acoustic biometrics. Our species-adaptive architecture processes vocalizations from 10Hz elephant rumbles to 4kHz puppy whines, paired with probabilistic visual matching that tolerates stress-induced appearance changes. This work demonstrates that AI grounded in biological communication principles can serve vulnerable populations that lack human language.
2603.24470.paper_summary_zh: 每年，有一千萬隻寵物進入收容所，與他們的家庭分離。儘管守護者和失散動物都在絕望地尋找，但70%從未重聚，並不是因為沒有匹配，而是因為當前系統僅關注外觀，而動物則通過聲音來識別彼此。我們問，為什麼計算機視覺將發聲物種視為無聲的視覺物體？根據五十年來的認知科學研究，顯示動物以近似的方式感知數量並通過聲音交流身份，我們提出了第一個整合視覺和聲音生物識別的多模態重聚系統。我們的物種自適應架構處理從10Hz的大象低吼到4kHz的小狗哀鳴的聲音，並結合了能夠容忍壓力引起的外觀變化的概率視覺匹配。這項工作證明了基於生物通信原則的人工智慧可以服務於缺乏人類語言的脆弱群體。
2603.24470.author: Badri Narayana Patro et.al.
